title: ranunculus aquatilis var. abrotanifolius
intro: Ranunculus aquatilis var. abrotanifolius, also known as water buttercup, is a subspecies of the water buttercup plant, which is a type of flowering plant native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Ranunculus aquatilis var. abrotanifolius include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin conditions, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
